# google-sites-clone

> Clone any Google Sites page to static HTML. Two-pass pipeline: SingleFile for CSS/images + Puppeteer for clean content.

## Docs

- [README](https://github.com/maximosovsky/google-sites-clone/blob/main/README.md): Quick start, features, tech stack
- [ROADMAP](https://github.com/maximosovsky/google-sites-clone/blob/main/ROADMAP.md): Development phases and decisions
- [ARCHITECTURE](https://github.com/maximosovsky/google-sites-clone/blob/main/ARCHITECTURE.md): Mermaid diagrams and system design

## Usage

```bash
npx google-sites-clone https://sites.google.com/view/your-site
```

## CLI Options

```
gsclone <url> [options]

  -o, --output <dir>   Output directory (default: ./clone)
  --no-images          Skip image localization
  --no-youtube         Skip YouTube thumbnail download
  --serve              Start local server after build
  --custom-nav         Use custom sidebar navigation
  --inline             Keep images inline (base64)
```

## Pipeline

1. **Crawl** (`lib/crawl.js`): Opens the Google Sites URL with Puppeteer, parses sidebar navigation links (`li[role="treeitem"] a, nav a`), discovers all pages, generates `page-map.json` with titles, URLs, depth, and slugified filenames. Supports Cyrillic transliteration.

2. **SingleFile** (`lib/singlefile.js`): Runs `single-file` CLI on each page URL. Captures full CSS, base64-encoded images, and layout. Skips already-downloaded pages. 3-second pause between pages to avoid rate limiting.

3. **Puppeteer batch** (`lib/puppeteer.js`): Extracts content in batches of 5 pages. For each page: navigates, waits for `section.yaqOZd` selector, scrolls to trigger lazy loading (40 iterations), extracts all `<section>` elements. Replaces `<iframe>` with `data-iframe-src` placeholder divs. 60-second pause between batches.

4. **Image localization** (`lib/images.js`): Matches Google CDN URLs from Puppeteer content with base64 images from SingleFile by position. Decodes base64 to local files (`images/img0001.jpg`). Replaces CDN URLs in content HTML. Skips images smaller than 200 bytes.

5. **Build** (`lib/build.js`): For each page, extracts `<style>` blocks from SingleFile HTML and content from Puppeteer HTML. Converts YouTube iframe placeholders to clickable thumbnails (`img.youtube.com/vi/{id}/maxresdefault.jpg`). Wraps in HTML5 document with footer badge. Generates `sitemap.xml` and `robots.txt`.

## File Structure

```
google-sites-clone/
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ bin/gsclone.js          # CLI entry point (Commander.js)
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ lib/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ index.js            # Pipeline orchestrator (5 steps)
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ crawl.js            # Step 1: Auto-crawl navigation
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ singlefile.js       # Step 2: SingleFile pass
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ puppeteer.js        # Step 3: Puppeteer batch extraction
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ images.js           # Step 4: Base64 в†’ local images
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ build.js            # Step 5: Merge + sitemap
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ site/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ index.html          # Landing page (gsclone.osovsky.com)
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ style.css           # Landing page design system
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ ARCHITECTURE.md         # Mermaid diagrams
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ ROADMAP.md              # Development phases
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ llms.txt                # LLM summary (this file's shorter version)
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ llms-full.txt           # LLM full reference (this file)
в””в”Ђв”Ђ package.json
```

## Dependencies

- `puppeteer` ^22.0.0 вЂ” headless Chrome for crawling and content extraction
- `commander` ^12.0.0 вЂ” CLI argument parsing
- `single-file-cli` (global) вЂ” full-page capture with CSS and base64 images

## Key Technical Details

- Google Sites uses SPA architecture вЂ” direct URL loading may fail for subpages
- SPA fallback: if a page fails to load directly, navigate from home and click sidebar links
- Puppeteer targets `section.yaqOZd` вЂ” Google Sites' content section class
- SingleFile captures CSS that Puppeteer cannot (inline styles, computed styles, fonts)
- Image matching is positional (CDN URL #1 maps to base64 image #1)
- Slugification supports Cyrillic в†’ Latin transliteration for filenames
- Output HTML uses `lang="ru"` (hardcoded, site was originally Russian)
